CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be created.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"Detailed issue description in Markdown. Do NOT repeat the title as a heading since it already appears as the issue's h1. Labels must exist in the repository.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"parent":{"description":"Parent issue number for creating sub-issues. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. The title appears as the main heading, so keep it brief and descriptive.","type":"string"}},"required":["title","body"],"type":"object"},"name":"create_issue"},{"description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Be specific about what functionality is needed.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_tool"},{"description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"details":{"description":"Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be created.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"Detailed issue description in Markdown. Do NOT repeat the title as a heading since it already appears as the issue's h1. Include context, reproduction steps, or acceptance criteria as appropriate.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"labels":{"description":"Labels to categorize the issue (e.g., 'bug', 'enhancement'). Labels must exist in the repository.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"parent":{"description":"Parent issue number for creating sub-issues. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. 2026-04-09T08:29:25.3505064Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-09T08:29:01.594537404Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"safeoutputs","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"body":"## Documentation Gap\n\n**Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.BillingBenefits`\n**Service directory:** `sdk/billingbenefits/Azure.ResourceManager.BillingBenefits/`\n**Triggered by:** PR #57779 by @haiyuazhang\n\n## What Changed\n\nThe billingbenefits management SDK was migrated from Swagger/AutoRest to TypeSpec (MPG migration). This migration added **8 new resource types** to the package that were not present before:\n\n- `MaccResource` / `MaccData` / `MaccCollection` (Microsoft Azure Consumption Commitment)\n- `CreditResource` / `CreditData` / `CreditCollection`\n- `CreditSourceResource` / `CreditSourceData` / `CreditSourceCollection`\n- `FreeServicesResource` / `FreeServicesData` / `FreeServicesCollection`\n- `ConditionalCreditResource` / `ConditionalCreditData`\n- `ConditionalCreditContributorResource` / `ConditionalCreditContributorData`\n- `ContributorResource`\n- `DiscountResource` / `DiscountCollection`\n\nThe pre-existing resources (`BillingBenefitsSavingsPlanOrderResource`, `BillingBenefitsSavingsPlanResource`, `BillingBenefitsReservationOrderAliasResource`, `BillingBenefitsSavingsPlanOrderAliasResource`) remain.\n\n## Gaps Found\n\n- **CHANGELOG empty for v1.0.0-beta.5**: The unreleased changelog entry has no content despite the significant TypeSpec migration adding 8+ new resource types\n- **README Key Concepts section is generic**: Only links to the Azure SDK intro page; does not describe any of the actual BillingBenefits resources (SavingsPlans, Reservations, MACC, Credits, FreeServices, ConditionalCredits, Discounts, Contributors) or what each is used for\n- **README Examples section lacks code snippets**: Only links to a generic samples page; no inline code examples demonstrating common operations like listing savings plans, getting MACC details, or managing credits\n- **No sample test files exist**: The `tests/` directory has no `Samples/` subdirectory and no `Sample*.cs` files, so no test-backed snippets are available to populate the README\n- **New resource types (MACC, Credits, FreeServices, Discounts, etc.) are completely undocumented**: None of the 8 new resource types added by the migration are mentioned in the README\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above\n\n### Step 1: Modify files\n\n**File 1: `sdk/billingbenefits/Azure.ResourceManager.BillingBenefits/CHANGELOG.md`** — Edit\n\nUpdate the `## 1.0.0-beta.5 (Unreleased)` section to document the TypeSpec migration and new resource types:\n\n```markdown\n## 1.0.0-beta.5 (Unreleased)\n\n### Features Added\n\n- Migrated package generation from Swagger/AutoRest to TypeSpec.\n- Added support for `MaccResource` to manage Microsoft Azure Consumption Commitment (MACC) resources.\n- Added support for `CreditResource` and `CreditSourceResource` to manage billing credits and credit sources.\n- Added support for `FreeServicesResource` to list and manage free service entitlements.\n- Added support for `ConditionalCreditResource` and `ConditionalCreditContributorResource` to manage conditional credits and their contributors.\n- Added support for `ContributorResource` to manage billing benefit contributors.\n- Added support for `DiscountResource` to manage billing discounts.\n```\n\n**File 2: `sdk/billingbenefits/Azure.ResourceManager.BillingBenefits/README.md`** — Edit\n\nReplace the generic Key Concepts and Examples sections with content specific to this package:\n\n```markdown\n## Key concepts\n\n### BillingBenefits resources\n\n`Azure.ResourceManager.BillingBenefits` provides management operations for the following resource types:\n\n| Resource | Description |\n|----------|-------------|\n| `BillingBenefitsSavingsPlanOrderResource` | Represents a savings plan order that groups one or more savings plans. |\n| `BillingBenefitsSavingsPlanResource` | Represents an individual savings plan within a savings plan order. |\n| `BillingBenefitsSavingsPlanOrderAliasResource` | An alias used to create or reference a savings plan order. |\n| `BillingBenefitsReservationOrderAliasResource` | An alias used to create or reference a reservation order. |\n| `MaccResource` | Represents a Microsoft Azure Consumption Commitment (MACC). |\n| `CreditResource` | Represents a billing credit associated with a billing account. |\n| `CreditSourceResource` | Represents the source of a billing credit. |\n| `FreeServicesResource` | Represents free service entitlements for a billing account. |\n| `ConditionalCreditResource` | Represents a conditional credit for a billing account. |\n| `ConditionalCreditContributorResource` | Represents a contributor to a conditional credit. |\n| `ContributorResource` | Represents a billing benefits contributor. |\n| `DiscountResource` | Represents a discount associated with a billing account. |\n\nFor more information about Azure savings plans, see [Azure savings plans overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/cost-management-billing/savings-plan/savings-plan-compute-overview).\n```\n\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\n\nCreate a new sample test file at `sdk/billingbenefits/Azure.ResourceManager.BillingBenefits/tests/Samples/Sample1_SavingsPlans.cs`:\n\n```csharp\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\n\nusing System;\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\nusing Azure.Identity;\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.BillingBenefits;\nusing NUnit.Framework;\n\nnamespace Azure.ResourceManager.BillingBenefits.Tests.Samples\n{\n    public class Sample1_SavingsPlans\n    {\n        [Test]\n        [Ignore(\"Only verifying that the sample builds\")]\n        public async Task GetSavingsPlanOrders()\n        {\n            #region Snippet:BillingBenefitsGetSavingsPlanOrders\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\n            var subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\n\n            // List all savings plan orders accessible to the subscription\n            await foreach (BillingBenefitsSavingsPlanOrderResource order in subscription.GetBillingBenefitsSavingsPlanOrdersAsync())\n            {\n                Console.WriteLine($\"Savings Plan Order: {order.Data.Name}, State: {order.Data.ProvisioningState}\");\n            }\n            #endregion\n        }\n\n        [Test]\n        [Ignore(\"Only verifying that the sample builds\")]\n        public async Task GetSavingsPlan()\n        {\n            #region Snippet:BillingBenefitsGetSavingsPlan\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\n            var subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\n\n            string savingsPlanOrderId = \"\u003csavings-plan-order-id\u003e\";\n            string savingsPlanId = \"\u003csavings-plan-id\u003e\";\n\n            // Get a specific savings plan order\n            BillingBenefitsSavingsPlanOrderResource order = await subscription\n                .GetBillingBenefitsSavingsPlanOrderAsync(savingsPlanOrderId);\n\n            // Get a specific savings plan within the order\n            BillingBenefitsSavingsPlanResource savingsPlan = await order\n                .GetBillingBenefitsSavingsPlanAsync(savingsPlanId);\n\n            Console.WriteLine($\"Savings Plan: {savingsPlan.Data.Name}\");\n            Console.WriteLine($\"Term: {savingsPlan.Data.Properties?.Term}\");\n            #endregion\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\nAdd snippet placeholders to the README `Examples` section:\n\n```markdown\n## Examples\n\n### List savings plan orders\n\n```C# Snippet:BillingBenefitsGetSavingsPlanOrders\n```\n\n### Get a savings plan\n\n```C# Snippet:BillingBenefitsGetSavingsPlan\n```\n\nFor more code examples, see [.NET Management Library Code Samples](https://aka.ms/azuresdk-net-mgmt-samples).\n```\n\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\n\nThe README at `sdk/billingbenefits/Azure.ResourceManager.BillingBenefits/README.md` currently has all required sections but several need content improvements:\n\n1. ✅ **Getting started** — present but install command uses `--prerelease`; keep as-is\n2. ⚠️ **Key concepts** — present but generic; replace with service-specific resource table (see Step 1)\n3. ⚠️ **Examples** — present but has no code snippets; add inline examples (see Step 2)\n4. ✅ **Troubleshooting** — present\n5. ✅ **Next steps** — present\n6. ✅ **Contributing** — present\n\nRemove the standalone `## Documentation` section (lines 35–41) as this is not part of the standard README template and its links are already covered by other sections.\n\n### Step 4: Validate\n\nRun these commands in order:\n\n1. `dotnet build sdk/billingbenefits/Azure.ResourceManager.BillingBenefits/`\n2. `dotnet test sdk/billingbenefits/Azure.ResourceManager.BillingBenefits/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 billingbenefits` — injects code from `#region Snippet:` blocks into README placeholders\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 billingbenefits` — no new public types were added but run to confirm API surface is current\n5. `dotnet format sdk/billingbenefits/Azure.ResourceManager.BillingBenefits/`\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n## Next Steps\n\n\u003e [!TIP]\n\u003e **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot**
